mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during periods of elevated inflation compresses profit margins as input costs outpace output pricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 60 percent of observations when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI increases above 0.4 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL MoM changes remains below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows when the threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show zero correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 10-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to overall output, signaling earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during periods of low policy rates signal temporary supply shocks rather than demand destruction, triggering mean-reversion trades that produce positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of cases when INDPRO falls and FEDFUNDS remains below 1.5 percent.
